name: Priority Task Queue with Workers
description: Implement a synchronous priority task queue with workers across two modules. Module queue_types.py must define: Task dataclass with fields id (int), payload (Any), priority (int, lower number = higher priority), created_at (float); TaskResult dataclass with fields task_id (int), status ('completed'|'failed'), value (Any), error (str, default ''). Module task_queue.py must import from queue_types.py and implement TaskQueue class with methods: submit(payload, priority: int = 5) returns task id (auto-incrementing from 1); register_worker(name: str, handler: Callable[[Any], Any]); set_default_worker(name: str); process_one() pops highest-priority task and runs default worker, returns TaskResult or None if queue empty; process_all() processes everything in priority order, returns list[TaskResult]; pending_count() -> int; cancel(task_id) returns True if removed before execution. Within the same priority, FIFO by submission order. Worker exceptions are caught and reported as failed TaskResult.
signature:
# queue_types.py
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any
import time

@dataclass
class Task:
    id: int
    payload: Any
    priority: int
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)

@dataclass
class TaskResult:
    task_id: int
    status: str
    value: Any
    error: str = ''

# task_queue.py
from queue_types import Task, TaskResult

class TaskQueue:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def submit(self, payload, priority: int = 5) -> int: ...
    def register_worker(self, name: str, handler) -> None: ...
    def set_default_worker(self, name: str) -> None: ...
    def process_one(self) -> TaskResult | None: ...
    def process_all(self) -> list: ...
    def pending_count(self) -> int: ...
    def cancel(self, task_id: int) -> bool: ...
tests:
def test_submit_returns_increasing_ids():
    q = TaskQueue()
    a = q.submit('x')
    b = q.submit('y')
    assert b > a

def test_priority_order():
    q = TaskQueue()
    q.register_worker('echo', lambda p: p)
    q.set_default_worker('echo')
    q.submit('low', priority=10)
    q.submit('high', priority=1)
    q.submit('mid', priority=5)
    results = q.process_all()
    assert [r.value for r in results] == ['high', 'mid', 'low']

def test_fifo_same_priority():
    q = TaskQueue()
    q.register_worker('echo', lambda p: p)
    q.set_default_worker('echo')
    q.submit('a', priority=5)
    q.submit('b', priority=5)
    q.submit('c', priority=5)
    results = q.process_all()
    assert [r.value for r in results] == ['a', 'b', 'c']

def test_worker_failure():
    def bad(p):
        raise ValueError('boom')
    q = TaskQueue()
    q.register_worker('bad', bad)
    q.set_default_worker('bad')
    tid = q.submit('x')
    r = q.process_one()
    assert r.task_id == tid
    assert r.status == 'failed'
    assert 'boom' in r.error

def test_pending_count():
    q = TaskQueue()
    q.submit('a')
    q.submit('b')
    assert q.pending_count() == 2
    q.register_worker('echo', lambda p: p)
    q.set_default_worker('echo')
    q.process_one()
    assert q.pending_count() == 1

def test_cancel_existing():
    q = TaskQueue()
    tid = q.submit('x')
    assert q.cancel(tid) is True
    assert q.pending_count() == 0

def test_worker_value_passed_through():
    q = TaskQueue()
    q.register_worker('double', lambda p: p * 2)
    q.set_default_worker('double')
    q.submit(5)
    r = q.process_one()
    assert r.status == 'completed' and r.value == 10
constraints: Task ids start at 1 and increase by 1 with each submit
FIFO ordering preserved within same priority
process_one returns None on empty queue, never raises
Worker exceptions become failed TaskResult with str(exc) in error
cancel only succeeds if task hasn't been processed yet
